title: Aureline terminal-session truth, host-boundary badge, shell-integration quality, transcript/live state, and capability-limit record
description: Boundary schema for the terminal-session truth record every execution-surface consumer reads when it needs to answer 'is this a live shell, a basic PTY, a captured transcript, or a restored snapshot, and what authority does it carry?'. The terminal-session record composes with - and never re-mints - the PTY-owner-class, terminal-session lifecycle-state, protocol-conformance-level, and shell-integration vocabularies frozen in schemas/terminal/session_restore_metadata.schema.json (the terminal-protocol seed). It also composes with the execution-context root from schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json, the workspace-trust state from the trust-decision packet, the admin-policy epoch, the remote-agent target identity, the credential-handle classes from the secret-broker boundary, the command-dispatch descriptor, the shared-control / shared-terminal grant rows from the collaboration session-authority contract, and the entry-restore truth-audit user-impact label set. Raw PTY bytes, raw escape-sequence payloads, raw command lines, raw environment bytes, raw clipboard bytes, and raw shell prompts MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs only. Adding a new enum member, a new optional field, or a new sub-record kind is additive-minor with a terminal_session_truth_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row.

terminal_session_truth_schema_version.description: Document-level schema version. Bumped on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, and safe in support / evidence / replay exports.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque ref to another record (terminal-protocol open record, execution-context root, trust-decision packet, admin-policy epoch, remote-agent target-identity witness, credential-handle class, command-dispatch descriptor, collaboration shared-terminal-control-metadata row, etc.). Consumers resolve refs through the owning registry.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

redaction_class.description: Redaction-class re-export of the terminal-protocol vocabulary. metadata_and_hashes_only is the default; broadened_capture requires an explicit approval-ticket ref.

host_scope_badge_class.description: User-facing host-scope badge that the renderer MUST display verbatim. Distinct from the ADR-0021 pty_owner_class so the badge can be more specific than the owner class (for example container_local_pty vs local_host_desktop_pty both project pty_owner_class = host_desktop). Every non-local badge requires the local-vs-remote boundary cue. host_scope_unknown_requires_review fails closed and forbids any mutating live action.

shell_integration_quality_class.description: Confidence label the host attaches to the session's shell-integration coverage. Projects the ADR-0021 protocol_conformance_level vocabulary plus an observed-signal confidence dimension. Every quality class outside the optional_full_signals row MUST degrade command-boundary navigation, prompt-zone focus return, and exit-status badges to a typed not_available_without_shell_integration chip rather than silently disappearing.

cwd_resolution_class.description: Confidence label for the cwd field on the session record. live_cwd_token_observed is only valid under a session that has emitted at least one cwd_change signal under shell_integration_signal_record; last_known_cwd_token_only carries the most recent cwd token from a prior signal but the session is currently idle / suspended / lost-transport. cwd_unknown_no_signals_yet is the honest answer for a session whose shell has not advertised cwd. The session MUST NOT paint a stale cwd as live; renderers MUST narrow the cwd badge to the matching label.

shared_control_state_class.description: Shared-control posture re-projected from the collaboration session-authority contract's shared_terminal_control_metadata row. Solo sessions resolve to shared_control_not_offered_solo_session; shared sessions inherit the inbound or outbound view-only or temporary-typing-grant posture. Renderers MUST NOT infer shared control from presenter / follow state under the follow-and-presenter contract; control is an explicit grant.

live_or_transcript_state_class.description: Truth label for whether the surface is rendering a live shell, a paused live shell, a captured transcript snapshot, or a restored transcript that is explicitly not rerunable. Re-projects the ADR-0021 terminal_session_lifecycle_state plus the ADR-0021 terminal_restore_level row so the renderer never paints a transcript as live or admits silent rerun authority on a restored pane. transcript_* values MUST forbid every mutating live-session action; only an explicit user-initiated fresh session under the command-dispatch boundary may regain write authority.

capability_limit_class.description: Closed vocabulary of capability-limit reasons the session may report at any moment. Renderers MUST narrow the affected affordance (scrollback navigation, find-in-scrollback, paste, host chrome accelerators, IME, output paging, transcript capture, mutating actions) to the matching typed chip rather than silently dropping the affordance. Multiple limits may be active concurrently; each is a separate row.

session_user_impact_label_class.description: User-impact class re-exported from the entry-restore truth audit. live_session_continued is admitted ONLY when live_or_transcript_state_class is one of the live_session_* values AND auto_rerun_admitted_on_this_record is false. transcript_* states forbid live_session_continued.

alt_screen_state_class.description: Whether the session is currently rendering on the alternate screen buffer. Alt-screen sessions MUST narrow scrollback navigation, find-in-scrollback, and copy-from-scrollback affordances to a typed chip; capturing the grid into a transcript while alt-screen is active requires a typed capability_limit_class entry.

raw_mode_state_class.description: POSIX termios mode (or ConPTY equivalent) the session believes the shell is in. raw_mode_no_line_buffer_no_local_echo forbids host-side line-buffered input affordances and forbids paste-then-run shortcuts that depend on bracketed-paste round-trip.

boundary_cue_visibility_class.description: Whether the renderer is currently displaying the local-vs-remote / container / managed boundary cue. boundary_cue_must_be_visible_but_responsive_fallback_collapsed is a typed degraded posture - the renderer MUST surface the cue in the responsive-fallback header rather than silently dropping it. Sessions whose host_scope_badge_class is anything other than local_host_desktop_pty MUST resolve to one of the visible_* values.

transcript_capture_posture_class.description: Whether the session is currently capturing a transcript and under what redaction posture. transcript_capture_broadened_with_approval_ticket MUST cite an approval-ticket ref. transcript_capture_paused_secret_class_intersected MUST cite at least one credential-handle class ref.

denial_reason_class.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary for terminal-session-record refusals. Every refusal fails closed and cites exactly one value.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event id vocabulary for the terminal-session-record family. Raw PTY bytes, raw command lines, raw env bodies, raw prompts, and raw URLs MUST NOT appear on any event.

terminal_protocol_session_open_ref.description: Ref to the terminal_session_open_record from schemas/terminal/session_restore_metadata.schema.json that this truth record projects. The protocol seed is authoritative for pty_owner_class, advertised_protocol_level, shell_family_declared, and the OSC-allowlist binding; this record narrows them into a host-scope badge, a shell-integration-quality class, and a live-or-transcript class.

remote_agent_target_identity_witness_ref.description: Remote-agent target-identity witness ref (ADR-0020). MUST be non-null when host_scope_badge_class is one of ssh_remote_host_pty / remote_workspace_agent_pty / managed_workspace_agent_pty / provider_side_remote_agent_pty / compatibility_bridge_remote_pty. Null on local / container / devcontainer host scopes whose target identity is not a remote-agent witness.

container_target_identity_ref.description: Container / devcontainer target-identity ref (the ADR-0006 / container-session record id). Non-null when host_scope_badge_class is container_local_pty or devcontainer_pty. Null otherwise.

execution_context_root_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0009 execution-context root the session resolved through. Required regardless of host scope; a session that cannot resolve a root MUST refuse to open and emit a denial under terminal_execution_context_root_unresolved on the protocol seed rather than minting a record without this field.

trust_state_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0018 trust-decision record governing this session at capture time.

identity_mode_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0001 identity-mode envelope at capture time.

admin_policy_epoch_ref.description: Ref to the admin-policy epoch in force at capture time. Null only in identity_mode = account_free_local with no policy pack bound.

command_dispatch_descriptor_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0016 command-dispatch entry that opened this session. Renderers MUST use this ref - never the shell escape sequence stream - to attribute kill / restart / paste / rerun authority.

shell_integration_signal_count_observed.description: Number of shell-integration signals observed since session_starting. Quality classes that claim signal evidence MUST report a count > 0.

cwd_token_ref.description: Canonical cwd token (ADR-0006). Required when cwd_resolution_class is live_cwd_token_observed or last_known_cwd_token_only. Null when cwd is unknown / unresolvable / redacted.

shared_terminal_control_metadata_ref.description: Ref to the collaboration session-authority contract's shared_terminal_control_metadata shared-object row. MUST be non-null when shared_control_state_class is anything other than shared_control_not_offered_solo_session or shared_control_state_unknown_requires_review.

auto_rerun_admitted_on_this_record.description: MUST be false on every record whose live_or_transcript_state_class is one of the transcript_* values. The truth contract forbids silent rerun on a restored pane regardless of any other field; an explicit user-initiated fresh session under the command-dispatch boundary mints its own terminal_session_record with its own session_record_id.

active_capability_limits.description: Set of capability-limit classes currently in effect. Renderers MUST narrow the matching affordance; an empty array means no limits are active.

transcript_capture_approval_ticket_ref.description: Approval-ticket ref required when transcript_capture_posture_class is transcript_capture_broadened_with_approval_ticket. Null otherwise.

transcript_capture_secret_class_refs.description: Refs to credential-handle classes from the secret-broker boundary intersected by the transcript at the moment the capture pause was triggered. Non-empty ONLY when transcript_capture_posture_class is transcript_capture_paused_secret_class_intersected.

scrollback_bound_lines_max.description: Maximum number of lines retained in scrollback at capture time. Null when transcript_capture_disabled_no_record. Renderers MUST surface the bound on transcript-export review surfaces so the reviewer never sees a transcript that silently exceeds the declared bound.

scrollback_bytes_estimate_max.description: Aggregate byte estimate of the scrollback at capture time. Null when transcript_capture_disabled_no_record. The exact bytes never project; raw bytes MUST NOT cross this boundary.

audit_event_refs.description: Ordered refs to terminal_audit_event_record entries on the protocol seed plus this contract's audit-event ids. Raw bodies never appear.

denial_reason_class.description: Closed denial reason when the record is published as a typed refusal (for example a capability_limit_unknown_requires_review row that fails closed). Null on admitted records.

allOf.description: Non-local host scopes MUST cite the matching target-identity witness and MUST render the boundary cue.
Container / devcontainer host scopes MUST cite the container target identity and surface the matching boundary cue.
Restored / captured / imported transcripts MUST forbid auto-rerun and MUST NOT claim live_session_continued.
live_session_continued user-impact is admitted ONLY on a live state with auto_rerun_admitted_on_this_record = false.
Shared-control postures other than solo or unknown MUST cite the shared_terminal_control_metadata row.
live_cwd_token_observed and last_known_cwd_token_only MUST cite a cwd token; the other resolution classes MUST null it.
transcript_capture_paused_secret_class_intersected MUST cite at least one credential-handle class ref.
Quarantined sessions MUST surface the matching capability limit and forbid live mutating action.
Unknown / pending-review state MUST forbid auto-rerun.
